emergency_definition: An active sprinkler discharge, a fire alarm going off, or a hood suppression system discharge.
emergency_routing_rules: Route to Mike at 214-555-0199; if no answer within 45 seconds, route to Sarah at 214-555-0342. If neither picks up, take a message and mark it urgent. Send a text summary to 214-555-0500 for all calls.
non_emergency_routing_rules: Hold until the following business morning and send a text summary to the office manager at 214-555-0500.
call_transfer_rules: On-call technicians have 45-second timeouts. During office hours, transfer to the front desk at 214-555-0100 with a 30-second timeout before taking a message.
integration_constraints: Use ServiceTrade; fire alarm inspection jobs can be created automatically; do not create sprinkler jobs automatically (must be handled manually).
after_hours_flow_summary: Emergencies (sprinkler discharge, fire alarm, or hood suppression discharge) are routed sequentially to Mike and Sarah (45s each) before taking an urgent message. Non-emergencies are held until the morning. All after-hours calls trigger a text summary to the office manager at 214-555-0500.
office_hours_flow_summary: Transfer all calls to the front desk at 214-555-0100. If they do not pick up within 30 seconds, take a message.
notes: Company is in the process of switching from ServiceTrade to FieldEdge, but no changes should be made to the integration yet.
